name: Rasmus Thunberg
headline: Operations & delivery lead bridging strategy and execution across customer data, MarTech, and identity at multi-brand scale
profile_summary: Operations and delivery lead with 2.5+ years of consulting experience driving customer data and MarTech delivery at scale. Currently leads the horizontal delivery of a portfolio of parallel customer data and MarTech initiatives across four H&M Group brands in 60+ markets — sitting at the bridge between global operations and local market execution. Strong analytical foundation combined with cross-functional delivery experience across Commercial, Legal, Architecture, and engineering. Heavy daily user of AI tools to compound own output and the processes built.

experience_blocks.company: Tata Consultancy Services / H&M Group — Portfolio Brands
experience_blocks.context: Own the horizontal delivery of a portfolio of customer data and MarTech initiatives across four global retail brands (COS, ARKET, &Other Stories, Weekday) operating in 60+ markets. The stream sits at the intersection of customer engagement, data, and platform operations — directly shaping how brands acquire, retain, and grow their customer base.
responsibilities: Bridge global operations with local market execution, coordinating customer data migrations and integrations across CRM, CDP, and Identity Lifecycle Management (ILM) systems as brands launch and localise in new geographies.

Define and document the operational playbooks and integration workflows that enable scalable adoption of new processes by engineering and brand-side teams — reducing onboarding friction and standardising how the stream operates across markets.
Influence and align cross-functional stakeholders across Architecture, Legal, Privacy, and Commercial — translating ambiguous, competing constraints into clear requirements and adopted decisions.
Track operational metrics and budget consumption across contributing teams, surfacing trends and risks to leadership before they escalate.
Drive quarterly roadmap planning and prioritization — balancing commercial goals against legal and regulatory constraints across multiple markets.
Use AI tools daily to design, draft, and accelerate the workflows owned by the stream.
outcomes: Led the rollout of a consent and first-party data architecture across COS and ARKET that materially expanded the marketable customer base — directly enabling more effective lifecycle engagement and retention activity.
Implemented an auto-opt-in strategy in close dialogue with Legal and Data Protection, materially expanding the addressable customer base.

experience_blocks.context: End-to-end delivery and project lead for ARKET's full e-commerce re-platforming, with direct focus on customer journey coherence and identity resolution.
responsibilities: Owned product backlog in Jira; led sprint grooming, sprint planning, and follow-up against delivery targets.
Translated customer journey and product requirements into clear, prioritised backlog items.
Coordinated deliveries between ARKET's e-commerce team, developers, and external dependencies — identified cross-functional risks and resolved blockers before they hit the timeline.
Facilitated Scrum ceremonies and workshops focused on delivery efficiency and cross-functional collaboration.
Partnered with UX, brand, and engineering to make trade-off decisions that protected the coherence and quality of the customer journey through a complex re-platforming.
outcomes: Led the rollout of 'Omni-id' — ARKET's identity resolution initiative — unifying fragmented customer data across online and offline touchpoints into a single customer profile, directly enabling more accurate personalisation, improved CRM targeting, and lower identity duplication.

experience_blocks.role: Data Analyst / Business Expert — Wholesale Data
experience_blocks.context: Owned analytics and dashboards for wholesale teams across H&M Group brands.
responsibilities: Owned end-to-end delivery of new dashboards for wholesale teams across brands — from requirements gathering with the business to data flows and Power BI visualisation.
Built Splunk dashboards to assess incident impact and support cost optimisation decisions.
Led change management for one brand's wholesale team adopting SAP, including end-user training and adoption follow-up.
outcomes: Delivered dashboards giving leadership real-time visibility on operational metrics and customer impact across brands.

experience_blocks.context: Operations and incident escalation under ITIL-based processes.
responsibilities: Led structured root-cause analysis on escalated tickets under ITIL-based processes — shifting reactive issue handling into preventive process improvement.
Built automated tracking scripts for throughput, output, and code quality.
outcomes: Turned reactive problem handling into preventive process improvement through structured root-cause analysis.

experience_blocks.context: Accelerated traineeship in project delivery and applied analytics.
responsibilities: Accelerated traineeship in project delivery (Agile/Scrum), structured requirements gathering, and applied ML in Python (Scikit-learn).

experience_blocks.context: Co-founded and operated a sustainability-focused service business (death-estate clearance and cleaning, with a strong reuse and recycling focus) end-to-end alongside full-time studies.
responsibilities: Direct ownership of customer acquisition, customer relationships, pricing, and operations.
Supplier negotiation and operational delivery from scratch.
outcomes: Built and ran a sustainability-focused service business end-to-end alongside full-time studies.

tone_rules.rule: No overselling — strategically honest framing only
tone_rules.rationale: Generic superlatives undermine credibility; specifics build it

tone_rules.rule: Show, don't tell — favour concrete examples over adjectives
Address genuine gaps transparently via adjacent experience
tone_rules.rationale: Hiring managers spot faked claims; honest framing converts better

tone_rules.rule: Always prefer numbers, names, and concrete artefacts over superlatives
Anchor headline claims on the named numbers: 4 brands, 60+ markets, 2.5 years consulting
tone_rules.rationale: Consistent specifics across applications signal the same person, not a chameleon

tone_rules.rule: Position as a bridge between business outcomes and technical/data systems — not a pure-PM and not an engineer
tone_rules.rationale: Both CVs lean into this framing because it matches the actual work

tone_rules.rule: Mention daily AI tool usage when the ad signals AI-positivity or modern delivery practices; drop it when the ad is conservative
tone_rules.rationale: It is a genuine differentiator but reads as posturing in old-school contexts

positioning_tensions.tension: Breadth (horizontal portfolio) vs. specialization (customer data / identity / consent)
positioning_tensions.default_lean: Lead with the specialization (customer data, consent, identity resolution) — breadth is the supporting backdrop
positioning_tensions.when_to_flip: Ad explicitly seeks generalist program/IT-PM coverage across portfolios — flip to the horizontal-delivery framing

positioning_tensions.default_lean: English-language ads in MarTech/data/product contexts → operations & delivery framing
positioning_tensions.when_to_flip: Swedish-language or traditional IT-PM ads (timplaner, risk, budget, leverans) → IT-projektledare framing with portfolio delivery as the headline

positioning_tensions.default_lean: Execution-led with strategic judgement (translating constraints into adopted decisions)
positioning_tensions.when_to_flip: Ad explicitly seeks strategy/advisory framing — lead with decision-shaping outcomes (consent architecture, vendor evaluation, roadmap balance)

positioning_tensions.tension: Tech depth vs. business outcome ownership
positioning_tensions.default_lean: Bridge — fluent in CRM/CDP/ILM systems, owns business outcomes
positioning_tensions.when_to_flip: Pure engineering role → do not lead with this; pure GTM/sales role → drop ILM/CDP detail in favour of customer-impact framing

positioning_tensions.tension: TCS consultant vs. embedded product/operations role
positioning_tensions.default_lean: Frame as embedded operator inside H&M Group (which is the lived reality) — not as detached consultant
positioning_tensions.when_to_flip: Ad explicitly values consultancy/advisory background — lead with the consulting frame and 2.5 years of structured delivery exposure

positioning_tensions.default_lean: Mention as productivity multiplier in profile and side-projects section
positioning_tensions.when_to_flip: AI-native / AI product company → foreground heavily, including side projects; conservative enterprise → keep brief and outcome-framed

education.institution: Lund University School of Economics and Management
education.focus: Specialisation in Data Analytics and Statistics

certifications.name: Plytix Certificate in Product Information Management (PIM)
